metrics.narrative: No hard quantified business-impact metric (hours saved, cost %) was captured from the buyer. Pricing targets were discussed internally (~$50k/port modeled 30 Dec 2025) and a $240k Northport-only proposal was lodged 17 Mar 2026; the group-level ask now being evaluated is roughly $150k/port x 7 (~$1.05M), unconfirmed. The buyer's own framing of value stayed at the process level (manual tender pain, payment automation) rather than a quantified ROI case.

champion.narrative: Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus (Head of Tender and Contracts) was a strong early champion — 'strongly leaning to Zycus, and anti Ramco' (10 Dec 2025 note) and secured internal approval to proceed with Zycus modules (29 Jan 2026). Her last confirmed engagement was an email reply on 30 Mar 2026 (~101 days ago); she is not mentioned in any Next Step entry after the deal moved to the MMC group level (1 Jun 2026 onward), raising risk that her influence has been bypassed or she has gone cold.

competition.narrative: Ramco is the incumbent P2P system and the dominant, best-evidenced competitive thread: it lacks e-bidding/tender functionality (buyer pain), flip-flopped on retention (29 Jan 2026: 'mgmt is looking to retain RAMCO P2P'; 9 Feb 2026: 'management for Northport have decided to drop RAMCO now- flip flopped'), and was 'pushing them hard' on the renewal (20 Jan 2026). Salesforce also logs iValua and SAP Ariba as competitors, but no call, note, or task in the read Avoma manifest corroborates either name — a hygiene gap, not a knowledge gap, since the field may reflect RFP-stage entrants not captured in the calls read.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well documented and buyer-voiced across multiple calls: manual tender/e-bidding processes with no digital tracking (Nazual Azma Reffin, 25 Nov 2025: 'I think it's quite urgent now since we're supposed to migrate to kind of transaction... tender is doing manually'), Ramco's lack of e-bidding/tender functionality, and Ramco's PO-level risk assessment being a poor fit versus Zycus iRisk (30 Dec 2025 internal note).

paper_process.narrative: A proposal was submitted 17 Mar 2026, revised into a 2-phase implementation, with concessions tied to a 31 Mar 2026 LOI and a full BOM to be signed by 7 Apr 2026. There is no evidence in the read Salesforce activity or Avoma manifest that either milestone was executed; both dates have lapsed roughly 13-14 weeks ago with no follow-up confirmation of signature.

economic_buyer.narrative: Two economic-buyer figures are named — the MMC group owner (Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary) and Northport's 'new CEO' — but neither has been on a Zycus-attended call in the read manifest. On 2 Jul 2026 the consolidated proposal reportedly reached the group owner with a positive reaction, per the rep's dated log; this is a strong access-to-power signal but is rep-reported, not independently verified by a direct Zycus-EB interaction.

decision_process.narrative: As of 6 Apr 2026, the process ran through Northport's IT/PM lead presenting a 2-of-3 pricing comparison to the new CEO for sign-off (no formal tender). By 1 Jun 2026 the process was restructured entirely: the opportunity moved under a new MMC-group entry to consolidate all 7 ports and take the proposal directly to the group owner. This is a material process change with no confirmed new approval chain or timeline yet.

decision_criteria.narrative: Detailed requirements were captured directly from the buyer across two major calls: full source-to-pay from requisition through billing and payment with automated finance workflows and a supplier portal (11 Feb 2026), end-to-end e-bidding/tender/RFQ/CLM with the relevant stakeholder intake and Ramco integration (25 Nov 2025), and a preference for Zycus iRisk scorecards over Ramco's PO-level risk assessment (30 Dec 2025 internal note reporting buyer feedback).

items.name: Forecast: Best Case → Pipeline
items.summary: Despite the stage and amount jump, forecast confidence was downgraded, indicating the bigger deal is seen as less certain to close — consistent with a reset evaluation rather than a straightforward upsell.

day_summary.overall: Big, contradictory day on paper: the deal advanced a stage to Formal Evaluation and the amount nearly tripled from 240K to 650K, but forecast was simultaneously downgraded from Best Case back to Pipeline — signalling this is a scope expansion still needing to be re-qualified, not a confirmed win. Combined with the prior slip from Vendor Selected back to Shortlisted and the close date pushed two months, the picture is a deal that got bigger but less certain, likely due to a re-scoped or re-opened evaluation on the buyer side. No inbound or outbound communication is recorded, so the driver behind these changes isn't documented — worth confirming what prompted the scope jump.

items.detail: Budget tolerance confirmation for the 7-port MMC group consolidation is pending (Next Step 2 Jul: 'Datuk Nural is now working channels to confirm budget tolerance for all 7 ports. Likely 1-2 weeks for responses'). The scope expanded from a Northport-only deal (~$240k) to a group-wide opportunity (~$150k/port x 7 = ~$1M), but the opp amount is still $650k and the budget is unconfirmed. The 30 Dec planning call noted budget constraints limited scope to core modules (iRisk and Anna held as optional). Without confirmed budget and EB sign-off, the deal cannot close.

The economic buyer is unmapped and unengaged. The Next Step names the MMC group owner and a Northport 'new CEO,' but neither is in contact roles or has appeared on calls. No CFO or finance executive is engaged on commercials, pricing, or ROI. The 12 Mar Next Step says 'internal discussion on next step (with CFO/CIO/CPO)' occurred, but it is unclear if this was a Zycus-attended meeting or internal buyer discussion, and no follow-up confirms EB engagement. Without EB sponsorship the deal cannot advance to the relevant stakeholder or close.

The buyer flip-flopped on the Ramco replacement decision: the 9 Feb Next Step states 'mgmt for Northport have decided to drop RAMCO now- flip flopped,' but the 29 Jan Next Step says 'mgmt is looking to retain RAMCO P2P, for short time while their contract expires.' The 20 Jan Next Step records 'Ramco pushing them hard (are aware to P2P side for them at risk).' The buyer's indecision and 3-month silence since 30 Mar suggest Ramco may have regained ground or internal alignment is unresolved.

The scope expanded from a Northport-only deal to a 7-port MMC group consolidation in June 2026 (Next Step 1 Jun: 'opportunity will move under a new entry for MMC- where we are trying to merge 7 ports and take a proposition directly to the owner'). This consolidation introduces multi-stakeholder approval complexity (budget tolerance checks across all 7 ports, group-level decision process) and decision delay. The opp amount ($650k) and stage (Formal Evaluation) do not reflect the expanded scope, and there is no evidence the consolidation has been formalized or the 7-port stakeholders aligned.

The original close target was 30 Mar 2026 (Next Step 6 Apr, 30 Mar entries); that date is now 4 months past. The current Aug 2026 close is a pushed placeholder with no confirmed path. The 30 Mar buyer email discussed a revised 2-phase proposal tied to a 31 Mar LOI that did not close. The buyer has been silent for 99 days since that email, and the rep's 2 Jul outreach has not yielded a buyer reply or next meeting. Without a forcing event or confirmed next step, the Aug close is not credible.

Champion (Siti) has gone quiet since 30 Mar 2026 (99 days ago, last buyer email; last call 9 Mar, 4 months ago). The 2 Jul Next Step does not mention her, instead naming a group-level stakeholder working budget confirmation, suggesting the locus of control may have shifted or she is blocked internally. Without champion re-engagement, the deal cannot restart momentum.

north_star_verdict.headline: Partner-relayed proposal reportedly reached the MMC group owner with a positive response, but Zycus has had no independently verified buyer contact in over 90 days and the deal is stalled awaiting group-wide budget tolerance confirmation.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline, with close date pushed beyond Aug 2026 pending confirmed group-wide budget tolerance and direct economic-buyer engagement.

flags: No quantified value case built — the buyer has articulated qualitative pain (manual tendering, no e-bidding) but no ROI, savings target, cycle-time reduction, or headcount impact has been captured. On a $650k-$1M deal (7-port consolidation), the EB will demand a business case to justify the budget. (Gap: quantified metrics and ROI; move: co-author a value case with Siti and finance, quantifying the cost of manual tendering and the savings/efficiency gain from Zycus S2P + AI, and use it to re-engage the EB.)

Buyer flip-flopped on Ramco replacement, signaling unresolved internal alignment — the 9 Feb Next Step states mgmt decided to drop Ramco, but the 29 Jan Next Step says mgmt is looking to retain Ramco P2P short-term, and the 20 Jan Next Step records 'Ramco pushing them hard.' The buyer's indecision and 3-month silence suggest Ramco may have regained ground or the decision is stalled. (Risk: Ramco retention; move: re-engage to confirm the Ramco replacement decision, validate the 7-port consolidation scope, and push the EB conversation to lock budget before Ramco's renewal cycle closes the window.)

Secure a direct Zycus-side introduction to the MMC group owner's office or the group-level budget/finance stakeholder rather than relying on a third-hand proposal hand-off, since no Zycus rep has been on a call with either party.

Re-verify Ramco's current incumbent status before assuming displacement is locked in; the buyer flip-flopped on retaining vs. dropping Ramco as recently as Feb 2026 and Ramco was actively pushing its renewal.

Reconfirm Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus's role and access now that the decision has escalated above Northport to the MMC group level, since she has not been referenced in the deal record since it moved to the group entry.

Competitive footing: incumbent Ramco flip-flopped on its own renewal stance and pushed hard on Northport in Jan-Feb 2026; with the deal now running at group level, Ramco's group-wide position is unverified and worth checking.

Multi-thread named contacts at the other 6 MMC ports now, while the group-wide budget tolerance decision is still open, rather than relying solely on Northport-level relationships and partner-relayed updates.

Realistic close date: the recorded 30 Aug 2026 close has no confirmed forcing event behind it after the original 31 Mar/7 Apr 2026 targets lapsed; anchor a fresh date to the budget-tolerance response expected around 16 Jul 2026.
best_practice_check.summary: The two live levers are re-securing the local champion thread and pressure-testing the incumbent's group-wide position, alongside anchoring a credible close date.

customer_preference.evidence: Strongest direct buyer-voiced preference is dated 10 Dec 2025 ('strongly leaning to Zycus, and anti Ramco') and has decayed significantly (~7 months old); the most recent signal (2 Jul 2026, MMC group owner's 'positive response and level of interest') is rep-reported, not a fresh buyer quote.

competitive_position.summary: Ramco (Northport's incumbent P2P system) is the real, best-evidenced competitive threat: it lacks e-bidding/tender functionality, flip-flopped on its own retention stance in Jan-Feb 2026, and pushed the buyer hard on renewal, while the buyer's own sentiment leaned anti-Ramco. Salesforce separately logs iValua and SAP Ariba as competitors, but no call or note in the read manifest corroborates either, so their current threat level is unverified.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: The deal has real, buyer-confirmed pain and requirements depth built over eight months, and a genuine access-to-power escalation is underway (proposal reportedly reached the MMC group owner on 2 Jul 2026), but the near-term Northport-only close plan collapsed when its 31 Mar/7 Apr 2026 paper-process targets lapsed, the champion has been silent for ~14 weeks, and the group-level budget is still unconfirmed.

deal_risk.text: Group-wide budget tolerance across all 7 MMC ports is unconfirmed, and the opportunity amount ($650k) does not yet reflect the ~$1.05M group-wide scope under discussion since 1 Jun 2026.
The recorded 30 Aug 2026 close date is a placeholder with no confirmed forcing event behind it, following the lapse of the original 31 Mar/7 Apr 2026 targets.

win_position.text: Deep, buyer-voiced pain and requirements were captured across five substantive calls (Nov 2025-Feb 2026): manual tender processes, Ramco's missing e-bidding capability, and a clear preference for Zycus iRisk scorecards over Ramco's PO-level risk model.

The proposal escalated to the MMC group owner (Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary) on 2 Jul 2026 with a reported positive reaction, and the deal owner's manager separately met the Group CEO of Theta Edge (MMC's ICT arm) on 11 Jun 2026 to walk through the proposal.
No direct Zycus-EB engagement exists with either the group owner or Northport's new CEO; every executive signal to date is rep-reported, not independently verified.

The champion, Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus, has gone silent since 30 Mar 2026 (~101 days) exactly as the deal shifted to a group-level process that bypasses her, risking loss of the one confirmed internal advocate.

deal_momentum.text: The last verified two-way buyer touch (an incoming email) was 30 Mar 2026, 101 days ago; all activity since is either a Zycus rep log or an unverified account of buyer reaction.
Seniority is rising: the deal moved from a Northport-only conversation to direct outreach at the MMC group owner and Theta Edge Group CEO level within the last 5-6 weeks.

The 31 Mar 2026 LOI and 7 Apr 2026 BOM sign-off milestones lapsed without confirmed execution, and no new concrete forward date has been set beyond a soft '1-2 weeks' budget-tolerance estimate from 2 Jul 2026.

customer_commitment.text: Internal buyer approval to proceed with Zycus modules was obtained by 29 Jan 2026, and the champion actively coached the deal owner on format and positioning ahead of the 6 Apr 2026 CEO pricing review.
Buyer indecision on retaining incumbent Ramco (29 Jan 2026 'retain' vs 9 Feb 2026 'drop') was never explicitly resolved on record before the deal moved to the group level.
